title: Hailey Storage Daybed with Back and Arms
summary: A daybed that adds a paneled back and two arms to a storage-bench base, turning a twin bed into a sofa-style daybed with drawers underneath.
description:
This is the upgrade that turns the Hailey storage bench into a full daybed: a tall paneled headboard along the back and two arm panels at the ends, so a twin mattress reads as a sofa by day and a bed by night, with storage drawers below. It builds directly on the Hailey Storage Bed bench, so build that base first — this plan adds the back and arms to it.

The defining detail is that the 2x4 legs are ripped down to 3" wide. Taking 1/4" off each edge squares up the rounded factory corners and gives the legs crisp, furniture-grade faces — but it means you need a table saw, and every dimension in the cut list assumes 3"-wide legs. If you skip the ripping, you'll have to re-figure the panel widths.

The headboard and arm panels are particle board framed in 1x2/1x4 and joined to the legs with pocket holes drilled every 8–12" along the back, set for 3/4" stock. Panels are big and floppy until they're fastened, so get a second pair of hands to hold them while you drive the screws. Assembly order matters: build the bench, the arms (which are essentially taller footboards), and the headboard separately, then join them — arms to the front bench first, per the source — before dropping in the slats.
timeLabel: 7–11 hrs on top of the bench base

tools.note: Required — the 2x4 legs are ripped to 3" wide, which every dimension assumes.
Panels join to legs with 3/4" pocket holes every 8–12".

cutList.note: Approximate count; cut to fit and space evenly.

steps.title: Build the storage bench base first
steps.body: This daybed sits on the Hailey Storage Bed bench, so build that base first following its own plan — the drawers and box come from there. Everything below adds the back and arms to it.

steps.title: Rip the legs to 3"
steps.body: Rip the 2x4 legs down to 3" wide, taking 1/4" off each edge to square up the rounded factory corners. This needs a table saw and every panel dimension assumes it — don't skip it or the widths won't work out.

steps.title: Build the headboard panel
steps.body: Assemble the headboard: frame the particle-board panel and join it to the ripped legs with 3/4" pocket holes drilled every 8–12" along the back, glue, and 1-1/4" screws. The panel is big and floppy — get a second person to hold it up while you drive the screws.

steps.title: Add the headboard top trim
steps.body: Add the bottom 1x4 trim and top 1x2 trim to the headboard panel, then cap it with the 1x3 top. Keep it square.

steps.title: Build the two arms
steps.body: Build the arms exactly like the headboard but to the arm dimensions — they're essentially taller footboards. Frame the panels, join to ripped legs, add the 1x2 arm trim and 1x3 tops. Remember the dimensions assume 3"-wide legs.

steps.title: Assemble and add slats
steps.body: With the bench, arms, and headboard built, assemble without the slats: attach the arms to the front bench first, then the headboard. Fasten the 2x2 slat cleat, level everything, then lay the ~14 slats and space them evenly. Sand and finish.

images.alt: Storage daybed with a paneled back and two arms over drawer storage
